picks.game: Tampa Bay Rays @ Boston Red Sox
picks.rationale: The Boston Red Sox are on an impressive 12-game winning streak, including a 7-6 victory over the Tampa Bay Rays yesterday and a doubleheader sweep on July 17. Boston's recent form shows a dominant 10-0 straight-up record in their last 10 games, while the Rays are struggling with a 4-game losing streak. The probable pitching matchup features Boston's Sonny Gray (11-1, 2.54 ERA) against Tampa Bay's Shane McClanahan (8-5, 2.83 ERA), giving the Red Sox a slight edge with their ace. Given their current momentum and strong home performance, the Red Sox moneyline at -126 is a high-confidence pick.

picks.game: Los Angeles Dodgers @ New York Yankees
picks.rationale: This matchup features two strong starting pitchers in Yoshinobu Yamamoto (2.85 ERA, 0.91 WHIP) for the Dodgers and Cam Schlittler (2.05 ERA) for the Yankees in Game 1 of a doubleheader. While Yamamoto had a recent rough outing, both pitchers generally boast excellent ERAs and WHIPs, indicating a potential low-scoring contest. The New York Yankees offense is currently in a 'deep slump,' having lost 7 of their last 8 home games. The combination of elite pitching and a struggling Yankees lineup supports the Under 9 total at -118.

Despite the Milwaukee Brewers winning yesterday's game and the last five head-to-head matchups against the Marlins, Miami has demonstrated strong performance on the road, winning 5 of their last 7 away games. The Brewers are currently dealing with several significant injuries, including Frelick on the 10-day IL and Woodruff undergoing shoulder surgery. These key absences could impact Milwaukee's performance, offering value for the Marlins at plus-money odds of +110 against a hobbled home team.

The Texas Rangers secured a 7-6 victory over the Atlanta Braves yesterday, evening the series at one game apiece and building momentum for today's rubber match. Rangers starter Nathan Eovaldi (9-7, 4.04 ERA) is set to return from an illness, providing a veteran presence on the mound against Atlanta's Grant Holmes (5-4, 3.61 ERA). With Ronald Acuña Jr. of the Braves potentially still limited or just returning from rehab, the Rangers' offense, which showed life yesterday, has a good opportunity to clinch the series on the road at -122.

The Chicago Cubs are facing extensive injury concerns, particularly within their pitching staff, with numerous key players like Hoby Milner, Ben Brown, Edward Cabrera, Daniel Palencia, Jameson Taillon, and Justin Steele all on the injured list. While some are nearing a return, the sheer volume of pitching absences significantly weakens the Cubs' overall strength. The Minnesota Twins, despite their sub-.500 record, are in a favorable position to capitalize on this depleted Cubs roster. The generous moneyline odds of +144 for the Twins present excellent value for an upset against a team with such profound injury challenges.

Spain enters the final with exceptional defensive form, having conceded only one goal across seven World Cup matches and securing six consecutive wins after an initial draw. Their momentum is strong, highlighted by a convincing 2-0 victory over France in the semi-finals. While Argentina has relied on extra time in three straight knockout games, Spain's disciplined approach and defensive solidity give them a significant edge to win in regulation. Key players Lamine Yamal and Pedro Porro, despite minor doubts, are expected to be fit for the crucial match.

Spain's defense has been outstanding throughout the tournament, allowing just one goal in seven games, which strongly indicates a low-scoring affair. World Cup finals are historically tight and cautious matches, with both teams often prioritizing defensive solidity over expansive attacking play. The bookmakers' odds also favor the Under 2.5, reflecting the expectation of a closely contested game. This defensive prowess from Spain, combined with the high stakes, points towards fewer goals.

Lionel Messi is confirmed active and a pivotal player for Argentina in today's final. He has been in phenomenal form throughout the tournament, leading with 8 goals and 4 assists. Even at 39, Messi remains the most influential player for Argentina, consistently delivering match-winning impacts. Given the high stakes of the World Cup Final, Messi will undoubtedly be central to Argentina's attack and is highly likely to find the back of the net.

As defending champions, Argentina has maintained a perfect 10-0-0 record in the tournament, consistently finding ways to win, even if it meant going to extra time in three knockout games. The head-to-head record between these two nations is perfectly even over 14 previous encounters, with six wins apiece and two draws, indicating a balanced rivalry. With Lionel Messi's exceptional form and ability to produce moments of brilliance, Argentina offers significant value as an underdog in this World Cup Final.

Despite Spain's strong defense, Argentina's recent tournament matches have been notably high-scoring, with their last five games featuring scores like 2-1, 3-1, 3-2, 3-2, and 3-1. Argentina's potent attack, spearheaded by Lionel Messi, has scored 29 goals this season, demonstrating their offensive capability. If Argentina manages to break through Spain's formidable defense, and Spain's own attacking talent responds, the game could open up, as seen in their 6-1 friendly match in 2018.
